replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: I have a treadmill motor and under load you can pretty easily spin it with hand and create much more than 12V. 180V DC motor in this case. I think it was made in USA before sold here in Finland. Perhaps you have different kind of motors if generation is difficult?

author: @captainprototype187
text: I am still working on my late 80s printer. It is an HP laserprinter. It is well put tpgether and a bitch to take apart. But I see some good stuff,,,,,,,,,, Ill keep chipping away at it!

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: Do printers of this age contain brushless DC motors?

replies.author: @captainprototype187
replies.text: @Teknopottu One or maybe 2. Still working on it. Many solenoids. Those are hard to find a use for.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: @captainprototype187 Nice to found something of use atleast 👍🏻

author: @Buzzhumma
text: Get yourself a small motorbike engine with gearbox. You can use the gearbox for all sorts of things and they have a inbuilt clutch. 80cc bikes are awesome and motorbike shops have far more stuff in the bin than you could possible imagine . Relationships 👍🏻

author: @3dmaker699
text: I love getting STUFF you can use from a junkyard.

author: @just_russ4633
text: I miss my local rubbish dump. I have been stuck in the Philippines for over a year and a half because of the virus. When I am at home in Australia I collect useful stuff everyday. I have a shed full of motors and electrical components.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: I envy your dump. Hope you get home soon. Here in Finland you are not allowed to take stuff from dumps, they are not even selling anything. They are just scrapping the materials and melting them for new. And we are adressed as a green country but reusing an repurposing stuff is made difficult. Atleast some cities have open containers for people to bring their metal junk in but not here in Kuusamo.

author: @malti2104

author: @craigglewis
text: I have 3 treadmills I got from the tip. Looking forward to this build. A go-cart, or a Mini EV. :) using all your tricks with Graphene of course lol

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: love the power of those motors, the output shaft size and I use their controllers also with an added heat sink. they make a good drill press motor for a slower speed drill press, lower speed helps you keep from burning up your drill bits, remember to keep them running cool with good air flow and filter out the dust going into the brush set area and lastly try to reduce the arcing with  condenser or two, it will make the brushes last longer especially when you call on the 2 horses it offers up depending on the motor imho luck to all

author: @benniedonald
text: I have been looking for a treadmill also. If I find one with actuators I plan to use them. Well I hope to use them with a remote. To bar my shed door from the inside. I will post a video of it if I ever finish it. The rain keeps me from working on it. LOL 😆 🤣

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Imagine (as you said) that motor on your flywheel or even combining your flywheel and vertical wind turbine with the motor and the coil / magnets. All up a dual output wind turbine. 
Invention is basically ideas and a attitude of not giving up when you fail because failures are the world's best teachers. I might have to develop a few social connections to get old treadmills, excercise bikes and the like so I can put these ideas to use.
By the way, yet another brilliant video Rob.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text:
After this video I started looking at Facebook groups (Buy, Sell and Swap) I am picking up a treadmill today and another tomorrow, both for free and in working condition. They just need some TLC apparently but you know what I really want them for.
I put an ad on asking for both treadmills and excercise bikes saying I need to get them to help with pain management (which is not a lie) I might be able to find one that can be used for this purpose.

author: @captainmidnite93
text: The Mrs's and I practice WISHcraft: picture what y'all are looking for clearly for a bit, then put it out of mind entirely & go about your business. And glory be there it is! (helps if it's trash day).

author: @morski_kapetan
text: I think that the major part of being an inventor is not only to have a good idea but to be persistent and refine all details until the invention is finished. For each finished invention there were millions of ideas that were 'imagined' in the heads of people, thousands of projects that were started and never finished.

author: @saiello2061
text: In the old days, local tips and scrapyards were great, you could roam around at will, now everything's regulated for health and safety reasons. Skips outside private houses are still worth a gander at and I always keep my eye out. Also there are some websites ( FreeCycle ) that give away free stuff if you want to collect.

Last week I asked to buy some foil  covered T.G 50 mil polyurethane insulation which had been removed from a wall as they are extending my Local Aldi Store It was in pristine condition but I was informed by the builder that he could not sell it to me as it had to be disposed off which entailed him hiring a skip and then a truck would collect it and transport it 80 + miles 
Is This MADNESS keeping our Planet Green

author: @William_Hada
text: Years back when I was a kid, Towns used to have local dumps, or tips. What a source of materials and repairable things they were! Many fond memories of bringing home all kinds of interesting and useful stuff!  LOL.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: @realfamilyman I hear you, brother. It's the same here in Finland. About 15 years ago you could go to local dump for electronics and metal stuff. 10 years ago just for metals. Now nothing is allowed. They don't even sell anything. You have to get in at night to get even the metal stuff, electronics are locked away. For the past decade only once I've get gaught but the employer just wished me happy holidays. They don't care too much if you don't make a mess. It's a shame that you have to break the law to repurpose and reuse stuff. And this is in a country that is supposed to be green.

author: @domesday1086
text: another great part of building local relationships is seeing the kinds of problems that are out there. Lots of industries out there with their own unique problems that you'll only hear about from the boots on site

author: @chrishayes5755
text: you should ask your mechanic friend for old differentials and transmissions.. there are very expensive and well machined gears in those for your projects.

author: @rickymcguire9493
text: As always you are on target with your ideas.  I always take your recommendations seriously.   Ok Ross I am subscribed . I expect interesting things.  Haha things like this is why I use a computer.

author: @BiosWars
text: Treadmills are everywhere in Canada, I've repurposed over 26 in the last year alone. It's truly a shame that people toss these after using them for a month or two.

replies.author: @chrishayes5755
replies.text: yea I've gotten my hands on a couple of them recently. lots a expensive parts to be had for free.. it's great!

author: @eastky1901
text:
I really need a wind turbine generater, small enough to fit onto my electric bike.on the front handle bar. I think 2 of these will charge my battery as I go. 
 The bike already had the wind resistance without the turbines mounted up front. So any energy will be positive. 
Also it might even reduce the wind resistance with 2 aerodynamic turbine generaters to use the wind rather than push it out the way.
I really don't know how to do this stuff.
I build things but they never work. I need your help. 
 Ps there's nothing in the market or I'd buy somthing

replies.author: @chrishayes5755
replies.text: I could be wrong but I think a wind turbine on a moving bicycle would create more drag (energy loss) than you'd gain back.

replies.author: @eastky1901
replies.text:
@chrishayes5755 I don't think so. The bike is already dragging without turbines. 
 Instead of just pushing the air out of the way, the two duel turbines will actually let the air go thru the blades then around the bike. The total resistance won't change. If anything it will get better with 2 bullet shaped objects on the front of my handle bars.

replies.author: @eastky1901
replies.text: @chrishayes5755 these turbines should be no bigger than 6 inches in diameter.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: @eastky1901 Perhaps you could use couple of brushless DC motors from big, old copy machines. Connect these into buck converter to get the desired voltage. If one motor does not generate enough voltage, connect them in series. Remember to use a diode to prevent battery from spinning the turbines. Your fins are pretty small to generate much power. But something like car radiator fans could work. Something like these spinners should be bolted pretty tight on handels and/or frame or they could be dangerous.

replies.author: @eastky1901
replies.text:
@Teknopottu hey man, you don't know how much you have helped me. I will try copy machine motors, and thanks for the diode tip, ( I don't know anything , only youtube information)   
 How about 2 razor motors.? There 250watt, 3 phase brushless motors. There bigger than I wanted, ( but so are copy machine motors I'd think) I already have the razor motors. If it may work, how do I funnel 3phase power into a dc battery?
